display.purpose: Instrument Serif for brand-led empty states and onboarding only.

headline.purpose: Geist SemiBold for major application headings.

title.purpose: Geist SemiBold for panels, dialogs, and family names.

body.purpose: Geist Regular for explanations and standard UI text.

label.purpose: Geist SemiBold for buttons, navigation, tabs, and metadata labels.

shadows.name: floating-control
shadows.purpose: Menus, popovers, and command palettes in dark mode.

shadows.name: blocking-dialog
shadows.purpose: Destructive confirmation and system-permission dialogs in dark mode.

shadows.name: dragged-item
shadows.purpose: Temporary lift while a row or file is being dragged.

motion.name: state-fast
motion.purpose: Hover, active, and compact selection feedback.

components.name: Primary Button — Dark
components.description: The single highest-priority action within a local decision area.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { height: 36px; padding: 0 12px; border: 0; border-radius: 8px; background: #D8D2BE; color: #20201C; font: 600 12px/1.35 Geist, Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; transition: filter 150ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), transform 150ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { filter: brightness(1.06); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #C6B98F; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button — Dark
components.description: A familiar bordered action for filters, menus, and secondary choices.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { height: 36px; padding: 0 12px; border: 1px solid #34342F; border-radius: 8px; background: #1D1E1A; color: #F1F0EA; font: 600 12px/1.35 Geist, Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #2C2D27; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #C6B98F; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Search Field — Dark
components.description: The standard catalogue search field with compliant placeholder contrast.
components.html: <label class="ds-search-wrap"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" aria-hidden="true"><circle cx="10.5" cy="10.5" r="5.5"/><path d="m14.7 14.7 4.3 4.3"/></svg><input class="ds-search" placeholder="Search font families"></label>
components.css: .ds-search-wrap { position: relative; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; } .ds-search-wrap svg { position: absolute; left: 11px; width: 16px; fill: none; stroke: #AAA9A0; stroke-width: 1.7; } .ds-search { width: 280px; height: 36px; padding: 0 12px 0 36px; border: 1px solid #34342F; border-radius: 8px; background: #1D1E1A; color: #F1F0EA; font: 400 14px/1.5 Geist, Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-search::placeholder { color: #AAA9A0; opacity: 1; } .ds-search:focus-visible { border-color: #C6B98F; outline: 2px solid #C6B98F; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Selected Navigation Item — Dark
components.description: The stable active state for primary application navigation.
components.html: Library
components.css: .ds-nav-selected { display: inline-flex; width: 180px; height: 36px; align-items: center; gap: 9px; padding: 0 12px; border-radius: 8px; background: #2C2D27; color: #F1F0EA; font: 600 12px/1.35 Geist, Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; text-decoration: none; } .ds-nav-selected svg { width: 16px; fill: none; stroke: currentColor; stroke-width: 1.7; } .ds-nav-selected:hover { filter: brightness(1.08); } .ds-nav-selected:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #C6B98F; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Status Chip — Dark
components.description: A compact, explicitly labeled system-state indicator.
components.css: .ds-status-chip { display: inline-flex; height: 24px; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 0 9px; border: 1px solid #34342F; border-radius: 22px; background: #131411; color: #AAA9A0; font: 600 12px/1.35 Geist, Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-status-chip svg { width: 13px; fill: none; stroke: #71B184; stroke-width: 2; }

components.name: Danger Button — Dark
components.description: The final action in an explicit destructive confirmation flow.
components.css: .ds-btn-danger { height: 36px; padding: 0 12px; border: 0; border-radius: 8px; background: #EA7970; color: #111210; font: 600 12px/1.35 Geist, Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; transition: filter 150ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-btn-danger:hover { filter: brightness(1.08); } .ds-btn-danger:active { filter: brightness(0.96); } .ds-btn-danger:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #C6B98F; outline-offset: 2px; }

narrative.overview: FontNest is a quiet, exact workspace built for long sessions among thousands of font faces. The frame borrows the discipline of an archival index: clear labels, stable rows, explicit provenance, and calm layers that let the typography under inspection remain the expressive material. It is professional and discerning without becoming sterile, nostalgic, or artificially cozy. The visual system is restrained by design. Dark Quiet Ledger is the selected reference mode; Light Quiet Ledger is its equal functional counterpart.
keyCharacteristics: Dense but breathable desktop information hierarchy
Familiar controls with explicit state boundaries
Geist for dependable UI; Instrument Serif only for controlled brand expression
The selected user font always owns the preview surface

rules.name: The Quiet Frame Rule
rules.body: If the interface competes with a font specimen, the frame is too expressive.

rules.name: The Mode Parity Rule
rules.body: Dark and light modes must expose identical information, component states, and safety cues. A feature is not complete until both modes are verified.

rules.name: The Ten Percent Rule
rules.body: Accent color occupies no more than 10% of a working screen. Its rarity creates hierarchy.

rules.name: The Semantic Signal Rule
rules.body: Success, warning, and danger colors communicate state only. Never use them for categories, branding, or visual variety.

rules.name: The Specimen Ownership Rule
rules.body: Real font previews always render in the selected user font. Instrument Serif is a brand voice and fallback specimen, never a substitute for inspected font data.

rules.name: The UI Sans Rule
rules.body: All controls, labels, metadata, navigation, and destructive warnings use Geist. Display serif in controls is prohibited.

rules.name: The Flat-by-Default Rule
rules.body: If a static panel needs a shadow to be understood, its tonal layer or border hierarchy is wrong.

rules.name: The One Plane Rule
rules.body: Never stack card inside card. Use dividers, spacing, or one tonal change to express internal structure.

dos: Do let inspected fonts provide the expressive typography while the application frame remains composed and restrained.
Do use the SVG logo as the canonical asset and preserve its exact path geometry, proportions, and clear space.
Do test every screen in both Quiet Ledger modes; mode parity is a release requirement.
Do keep one primary action per local decision area and reserve accent color for action, selection, and state.
Do label system fonts, app-managed files, duplicates, conflicts, and destructive boundaries explicitly.
donts: Don't resemble a generic SaaS analytics dashboard.
Don't resemble a novelty font marketplace.
Don't become an overdecorated creative tool whose branding competes with the user's typefaces.

Don't use gratuitous cards, nested cards, ornamental typography in controls, excessive motion, decorative glass effects, or unfamiliar interaction patterns where standard desktop affordances are clearer.
Don't make system fonts appear casually removable or hide the difference between system and app-managed files.
Don't use visual charm to soften a destructive action.
